skill.name: deep-reading-analyst
skill.description: Structured deep reading framework for articles, papers, reports, and long-form content. It applies SCQA, 5W2H, critical thinking, inversion, mental models, first principles, systems thinking, six hats, and cross-source comparison.
skill.summary: Analyze long-form content with structured thinking frameworks and practical synthesis.
skill.author: YYH211

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found markdown templates and analytical reference text rather than executable code. The Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and C2 hits are false positives from fenced markdown, file names, framework examples, and security-related vocabulary. No prompt injection, credential access, hidden network endpoint, or command execution behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The referenced locations contain fenced markdown templates, diagrams, and reference-file names, not Ruby backtick execution or runnable shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show markdown fences and templates in documentation files. I found no executable scripts, shell command invocation, or user-input command path in these locations.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Matches Are Analytical Vocabulary
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and C2-style findings are false positives. The cited content discusses reading frameworks, system archetypes, technology concepts, or SCQA section labels, not cryptographic code, scanning, exfiltration, or command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose and structural examples. The surrounding files are markdown reference materials, and no code path performs network discovery, cryptography, or remote control.

content.value_statement: Long articles and papers can hide weak arguments, missing evidence, and unclear implications. This skill applies structured thinking frameworks to extract meaning, critique claims, and turn reading into usable insight.
actual_capabilities: Breaks articles and papers into thesis, structure, evidence, assumptions, and implications.
Applies SCQA, 5W2H, critical thinking, inversion, mental models, first principles, systems thinking, and six hats.
Produces study notes, insight summaries, argument critiques, and action-oriented reading outputs.
Compares multiple sources to find agreement, disagreement, gaps, and synthesis opportunities.
Adapts analysis depth from quick summaries to detailed research workflows.
limitations: It depends on the quality and completeness of the source material supplied by the user.
It does not replace domain experts for legal, medical, financial, or scientific decisions.
Web research requires an available browsing tool and user permission in the host environment.
Very long documents may need chunking before full cross-source synthesis.

use_cases.title: Research Paper Understanding
use_cases.description: Turn a dense paper into a clear explanation of claims, evidence, assumptions, limitations, and follow-up questions.

use_cases.title: Strategy Article Evaluation
use_cases.description: Assess whether a business or strategy article has a sound argument, useful evidence, and practical implications.

use_cases.title: Multi-Source Synthesis
use_cases.description: Compare several sources on one topic and identify consensus, disagreement, blind spots, and a usable integrated view.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Article Breakdown
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this article at quick depth. Identify the core thesis, main structure, three key insights, and one practical action.

prompt_templates.title: Argument Quality Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use SCQA and critical thinking to evaluate this piece. Show the situation, complication, question, answer, evidence strength, assumptions, and missing counterarguments.

prompt_templates.title: Deep Framework Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this content deeply using 5W2H, inversion thinking, mental models, and first principles. Focus on hidden risks, root ideas, and practical applications.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Source Research Synthesis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these sources on the same topic. Map their claims, evidence, disagreements, unique contributions, unresolved questions, and a synthesized conclusion.

output_examples.input: A user provides a long essay about remote work productivity.
output: Core thesis: Remote work improves focus but weakens informal coordination unless teams redesign communication.
Strong evidence: The article separates individual output from team coordination and gives concrete examples.
Missing evidence: It does not compare results across job types, company sizes, or management styles.
Practical action: Test remote work policies by team workflow, not by one company-wide rule.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether a startup advice article is reliable.
output: SCQA result: The article frames startup failure as a mismatch between market needs and founder assumptions.
Critical concern: The evidence relies on memorable examples rather than broad failure data.
Inversion insight: The advice fails when teams copy tactics without validating their own market conditions.
Follow-up question: Which claims are supported by data rather than founder anecdotes?

output_examples.input: A user shares three sources about AI in education.
output: Agreement: All sources see AI tutoring as useful for feedback and practice.
Disagreement: They differ on assessment integrity, teacher workload, and student dependency risks.
Synthesis: AI is most credible as supervised support, not as a full replacement for instruction.
Research gap: More evidence is needed on long-term learning outcomes across student groups.

best_practices: State your goal and desired depth before analysis so the skill selects the right frameworks.
Provide the full text or stable excerpts when possible, especially for detailed argument review.
Ask for both insight and application when you need decisions, notes, or writing support.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for every framework when a quick summary is enough.
Do not treat the output as expert verification without checking important claims.
Do not provide only a title or vague topic when you need source-specific analysis.

faq.question: What types of content can this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It works best with articles, research papers, reports, essays, case studies, and other long-form content.

faq.question: Can it compare multiple sources?
faq.answer: Yes. It can map agreement, disagreement, evidence quality, unique contributions, and unresolved questions across sources.

faq.question: Does it browse the web automatically?
faq.answer: No. Web research depends on the host tool and user permission. The skill can still analyze text provided directly.

faq.question: Which frameworks are included?
faq.answer: It includes SCQA, 5W2H, critical thinking, inversion, mental models, first principles, systems thinking, six hats, and comparison matrices.

faq.question: Is it suitable for technical papers?
faq.answer: Yes, but technical accuracy still depends on source quality and domain expertise. Use it to structure understanding and questions.

faq.question: How should I get the best result?
faq.answer: Provide the source text, your reading goal, preferred depth, and any questions you need answered.
